prompt: We are a US SaaS company with Brazilian customers. Our app collects name, email, CPF, and purchase history. Do we need to comply with LGPD? Which legal bases apply to our processing?
assertions: Confirms LGPD applies extraterritorially via Art. 3 (processing with the purpose of offering goods or services to individuals located in Brazil, or data collected in Brazil), so the US SaaS company must comply

Maps processing to appropriate legal bases: contract performance (Art. 7, V) for account/service data and purchase history, with consent (Art. 7, I) or legitimate interest (Art. 7, IX — with a documented balancing test) for marketing/analytics uses

Treats CPF as regular (non-sensitive) personal data — the Art. 5, II sensitive-data list (racial/ethnic origin, religious belief, political opinion, union membership, health, sex life, genetic/biometric data) does not include tax IDs or financial data — while noting it still warrants strong protection

Identifies core operational obligations: transparent privacy notice (Art. 9), data subject rights handling (Art. 18), and appointing an Encarregado/DPO (Art. 41) — correctly noting ANPD Resolution CD/ANPD 2/2022 exempts small processing agents from formal appointment but still requires a contact channel

Flags international transfer compliance (Art. 33) since data flows to the US, which has NO ANPD adequacy decision (as of 2026 only the EU/EEA holds mutual adequacy with Brazil), so SCCs or another Art. 33 mechanism is required
